news.t: J.P. Morgan launches two actively managed equity ETFs — what you need to know

holdings.infoBox: JIG is an equity ETF with a total of 105 individual holdings. The top holdings are Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company stock at 6.09%, Samsung Electronics at 4.87%, ASML Holding at 3.39%, Safran at 2.60%, and SK Square at 2.57%.

overview.description: The JPMorgan International Growth ETF (JIG) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund is an actively-managed portfolio of global ex-US stocks fundamentally selected for growth and quality traits. JIG was launched on May 20, 2020 and is issued by JPMorgan Chase.

data.title: 4 Promising Active ETFs
data.text: These funds are flying under investors' radar.

data.title: International investing surges to start the year. What's ahead in 2021
data.text: Jeremy Schwartz of WisdomTree Asset Management, Bryon Lake of J.P. Morgan Asset Management and Tom Lydon of ETF Trends discuss the trends driving money to overseas assets.

data.title: J.P. Morgan launches two actively managed equity ETFs — what you need to know
data.text: J.P. Morgan's new actively managed ETFs are transparent and meant to bring investors income while mitigating market volatility, says the firm's head of Americas ETF distribution.

data.title: JPMorgan launches actively managed ETFs. Here's what you need to know
